title: Niagara

jokes.text: Pam is constantly throwing up because of the pregnancy. If she eats something the fetus doesn't like, she is screwed.
jokes.setup: Pam asking people to be considerate about smells
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact description of pregnancy's harsh realities
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about pregnancy realities, well-integrated with Jim's deadpan delivery style

jokes.text: It's amazing, a three-ounce fetus is calling the shots. It's so badass.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how pregnancy affects Pam's sensitivities
jokes.punchline: Jim calling an unborn fetus 'badass' for controlling the office environment
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect economy - calling a fetus 'badass' is unexpectedly hilarious and very Jim-like

jokes.text: Oh, gee. I'm sorry. I guess I missed the meeting where we all voted for you to get pregnant.
jokes.setup: Pam asking Phyllis to change her soap
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's sarcastic response about voting on pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic comeback that captures office politics and Phyllis's passive-aggressive nature

jokes.text: All morning I look forward to my afternoon cigars. And I am not stopping for anybody.
jokes.setup: Office discussion about accommodating Pam's pregnancy sensitivities
jokes.punchline: Creed claiming he smokes cigars in the office and won't stop
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed absurdity - the idea he's smoking cigars at work all along is both shocking and believable for his character

jokes.text: Watching people get sick always makes me sick. And, frankly, so does talking about it. So, wow.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Pam's morning sickness
jokes.punchline: Michael's chain reaction of nausea from even discussing sickness
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael self-focused response, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Well, what do you think? I spent all morning doing it. It is really special. Yeah, but aren't you supposed to do that to our... No. It's great.
jokes.setup: Pam showing Jim something she worked on
jokes.punchline: Jim's confused reaction suggests she did something to the wrong item/person
jokes.explanation: Subtle cringe moment where Jim realizes Pam's effort was misdirected but tries to be supportive

jokes.text: Believe it or not, Kevin, firecrackers are in the 'Don't' column.
jokes.setup: Wedding planning discussion
jokes.punchline: Jim having to explicitly tell Kevin that explosives aren't appropriate for weddings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his complete misunderstanding of wedding appropriateness, delivered deadpan by Jim

jokes.text: Well, you're lucky to have a grandmother. Some of us have to be our own grandmother.
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning her conservative grandmother
jokes.punchline: Angela's bizarre claim about being her own grandmother
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Angela line that's both nonsensical and perfectly captures her self-righteous martyrdom complex

jokes.text: What happens in Niagara stays in Niagara.
jokes.setup: Generic Vegas catchphrase setup
jokes.punchline: Kevin applying it to Niagara Falls
jokes.explanation: Basic adaptation of Vegas slogan - functional but unoriginal, though fits Kevin's simple humor

jokes.text: Don't, don't, don't. You stole my joke. Don't steal my joke. No, I didn't steal your joke. Yes, I said that yesterday.
jokes.setup: Kevin saying the Niagara line
jokes.punchline: Andy's petty insistence that Kevin stole his obvious joke
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's desperate need for credit and Kevin's obliviousness

jokes.text: So I will see you up there in Viagra Falls.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be authoritative about the wedding trip
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Niagara as Viagra
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism, predictable but well-executed for character consistency

jokes.text: Pam and Jim's wedding will be the single best pickup destination in the history of the universe.
jokes.setup: Wedding planning context
jokes.punchline: Dwight's over-the-top assessment of wedding pickup potential
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight hyperbole showing his single-minded approach to romance opportunities

jokes.text: For instance, Pam's cousin, Jocelyn Webster. There's a name. Two years ago, she was selling a mountain bike.
jokes.setup: Dwight promising detailed research on wedding guests
jokes.punchline: His 'research' is just a random Craigslist posting about a bike
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - Dwight's 'deep research' is hilariously shallow and useless information

jokes.text: Is that all you have on her? Well, if this is in fact her, because it's a very common name. Okay, you're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for more details about Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: Dwight admitting he's not even sure it's the same person, Michael's blunt response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's incompetence with Michael's rare moment of clarity

jokes.text: I thought, 'How can I take it to the next level?' The hair. It's the hair.
jokes.setup: Kevin talking about improving his appearance for the wedding
jokes.punchline: His solution is doing something terrible to his hair (implied visual)
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup where Kevin's confident about a presumably bad hair decision

jokes.text: We feel like we're in a limo and you're our driver.
jokes.setup: Michael asking someone to sit up front with him
jokes.punchline: Kelly treating Michael like hired help rather than a friend
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how people casually dismiss Michael's need for companionship

jokes.text: Not only is Erin really sweet and cute, she smells like my mom.
jokes.setup: Michael's attraction to Erin
jokes.punchline: The deeply uncomfortable mom comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate overshare that's both sweet and deeply uncomfortable

jokes.text: Click. You blinked. Damn it, now that's in my brain forever. Lousy picture! We should have hired a professional to take the mental pictures.
jokes.setup: Pam's aunt suggesting they take mental pictures
jokes.punchline: Jim treating mental pictures like real photography with all its problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Jim moment taking the mental picture concept to logical extremes with perfect execution

jokes.text: Hello. My name is Dwight Schrute. If you are listening to this, you are a lucky woman Michael has seduced.
jokes.setup: Dwight making Michael a romantic mix CD
jokes.punchline: Opening with his own bizarre narration for seduced women
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight delusion about romance combined with his complete misunderstanding of mood-setting

jokes.text: Hey, I got the room the night before you guys. I'll break in the bed.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam asking about their honeymoon suite
jokes.punchline: Packer's crude comment about using their bed first
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Packer humor, in-character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm afraid we're all sold out. Dwight Schrute. I have a reservation. Confirmation number, Romeo-Tango-G-seven-seven-four-five.
jokes.setup: Michael being turned away for no reservation
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately getting his room with military precision
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Dwight's efficiency versus Michael's poor planning

jokes.text: If I have a woman up there and things are getting amorous, she's going to get turned off if she looks in the bathroom and she sees you brushing your teeth with butter and clay, okay?
jokes.setup: Michael asking to stay in Dwight's room
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre description of Michael's hygiene routine
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Dwight knowledge of Michael's weird habits, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: When Mary was denied a room at the inn, Jesus was born. When Michael was denied a room at the inn, we don't know what happens because that story hasn't been told yet.
jokes.setup: Michael being denied a hotel room
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Mary and Joseph with messianic implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael self-aggrandizement mixing religious references with his delusions of importance

jokes.text: A queen-sized bed is five feet wide. I am not five feet wide, Michael. I'm not a physics major, Stanley. I'm just saying, be careful.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting he could share Stanley's bed
jokes.punchline: Stanley's deadpan correction and Michael's non-sequitur physics comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley deadpan delivery with Michael's typical non-logical response

jokes.text: She's the only 80-year-old with no smile wrinkles.
jokes.setup: Identifying Pam's conservative grandmother
jokes.punchline: Visual description implying she never smiles
jokes.explanation: Clever visual joke that efficiently conveys character through physical description

jokes.text: I wasn't sure about your branch of the family after I heard about your parents' divorce. But you and Jim are just perfect.
jokes.setup: Meeting Pam's conservative grandmother
jokes.punchline: Immediately bringing up family divorce as a character judgment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment establishing Meemaw's judgmental nature immediately

jokes.text: Well, I wouldn't care to live if I thought that.
jokes.setup: Pam saying nobody's perfect
jokes.punchline: Meemaw's dark response about not wanting to live
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark humor that perfectly captures religious perfectionism taken to extreme

jokes.text: The bad man was busy kissing the boss man's butt. Ew! That's right. It is ew. It's very ew.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining office politics to a child
jokes.punchline: Child's disgusted reaction and Dwight's agreement
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Dwight's inappropriate workplace explanations to children

jokes.text: She thought I was your boyfriend. You thought I was dating this? What the hell is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Sister mistaking Kevin for Oscar's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Oscar's horrified reaction to the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy, though Oscar's indignation is somewhat mean-spirited

jokes.text: In 1996, her 10th grade volleyball team went ten and two. What am I supposed to do with that, Dwight? That's a very good record.
jokes.setup: Dwight providing intel on wedding guests
jokes.punchline: His research is completely useless sports statistics
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Dwight's completely misguided research priorities

jokes.text: Hey, what is the deal with the Smart car? How smart is that? Those things are tiny.
jokes.setup: Michael doing uninvited standup comedy
jokes.punchline: Bad Seinfeld-esque observational humor about Smart cars
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad comedy showing Michael's poor comedic instincts and timing

jokes.text: The real smart car is KITT, from Knight Rider. That's a car that can talk. Can Smart car talk? Nope. That's not smart.
jokes.setup: Continuing Smart car bit
jokes.punchline: Michael's logic that talking cars are actually smart
jokes.explanation: Michael's childlike logic is somewhat endearing but still weak comedy material

jokes.text: Especially the Florida cousins, who obviously can't take a hint.
jokes.setup: Jim thanking people who traveled far for the wedding
jokes.punchline: Backhanded gratitude suggesting they weren't really wanted
jokes.explanation: Good wedding toast humor with Jim's subtle roasting of unwanted relatives

jokes.text: Pam, I can now admit in front of friends and family that I do know how to make a photocopy. Didn't need your help that many times.
jokes.setup: Jim's wedding toast about their early relationship
jokes.punchline: Revealing his photocopy incompetence was an excuse to talk to Pam
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to series history with sweet revelation about Jim's early romantic strategy

jokes.text: I've been driving stick since high school.
jokes.setup: Jim admitting to fake incompetence
jokes.punchline: Another revelation about pretending to need Pam's help
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to photocopy admission, building the pattern of Jim's sweet deceptions

jokes.text: What's obvious? Why can't Pam drink? Pam can't drink?
jokes.setup: Jim's casual mention of obvious reasons Pam can't drink
jokes.punchline: Guests realizing the pregnancy secret is being revealed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment building tension toward the big reveal

jokes.text: She shouldn't because she's an alcoholic. Pam is an alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Jim panicking about revealing pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Making up an even worse lie about Pam being an alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Jim's attempted cover-up is infinitely worse than the truth

jokes.text: Hey, what I think Jim is trying to say is that they had an accident.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam's pregnancy revelation chaos
jokes.punchline: Michael trying to help by calling the pregnancy an 'accident'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael making things worse while trying to help

jokes.text: And you know what? You can't expect them to be careful every time, because, frankly, it's just a different sensation.
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate wedding speech about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Getting into explicit details about contraception at a family wedding
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriate speech-making, perfectly in character but painfully awkward

jokes.text: The hotel's television set had a movie on called Brüno last night. The remote control had so darn many buttons on it, I couldn't turn it off. So, I had to just sit there while it happened to me.
jokes.setup: Meemaw explaining why she's leaving
jokes.punchline: Her description of being forced to watch the inappropriate movie
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with Meemaw's innocent description of being traumatized by inappropriate content

jokes.text: Oh! You're that foul man that kept talking about intercourse.
jokes.setup: Michael approaching Meemaw to apologize
jokes.punchline: Her blunt identification of him by his inappropriate speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Meemaw's direct, conservative way of addressing Michael's behavior

jokes.text: In fact, for the longest time my best friend was my grandmother. And then she met Harriet, and now she thinks she's better than everybody.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he's great with old women
jokes.punchline: His grandmother 'friend-dumping' him for another friend
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly pathetic Michael backstory showing his loneliness extends to elderly friendship dynamics

jokes.text: And I know, in your day, she would be considered a whore. But now, women go out and they have sex, and they get wild, and they take their tops off.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to convince Meemaw about modern attitudes
jokes.punchline: Using the worst possible language and examples for a conservative grandmother
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriate persuasion attempt, perfectly in character but painfully misguided

jokes.text: They're going to name the baby after you, you know. They're going to call it Meemaw. You mean Sylvia. Yes. And if it's a boy they will call it Silvio.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to convince Meemaw to stay
jokes.punchline: His assumption that 'Meemaw' is her actual name and creating 'Silvio'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael logic fail with the absurd 'Silvio' invention showing his complete misunderstanding

jokes.text: No one from the office has been to a real Bernard throw-down. If I was a girl, I would seriously reconsider coming to this party. But don't tell them that I said that because I want them there.
jokes.setup: Andy promoting his party
jokes.punchline: Admitting it would be unsafe for women while still wanting them to come
jokes.explanation: Good Andy self-contradictory logic showing his obliviousness to his own warnings

jokes.text: Looks like you're going to a fish fry, Dwight. No, they're howling at the moon. It's suggestive to women because of howling during sex.
jokes.setup: Comments on Dwight's outfit
jokes.punchline: Dwight's explanation of his shirt's supposed sexual appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight delusion about what's attractive, with his typical over-explanation

jokes.text: Yeah, but that's easy enough for you to say, Oscar. You have that thick, beautiful Chicano hair. So nice.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Kevin's hair concerns
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inappropriate racial comment delivered as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Typical Kevin inappropriate comment that's meant kindly but comes out wrong

jokes.text: Nine and three-quarters. I invented a device called 'burger on the go.' It allows you to obtain six regular-sized hamburgers or twelve sliders from a horse without killing the animal.
jokes.setup: Question about Dwight's horses
jokes.punchline: His bizarre invention for harvesting horse meat without killing the horse
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Dwight absurdity - the impossible logistics and casual presentation make this exceptional

jokes.text: Sears said no.
jokes.setup: Listing companies considering his horse burger device
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery that Sears rejected this insane invention
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that makes the absurd seem mundane through Dwight's disappointed delivery

jokes.text: I bawl the entire time. I cannot watch Pixar.
jokes.setup: Michael listing emotional Pixar movies
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to handle children's movies emotionally
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's emotional vulnerability in unexpected contexts

jokes.text: I found twins. Oh, my God. Twins. Aren't they magnificent? They're men, Dwight.
jokes.setup: Dwight excitedly finding twins at the wedding
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm is for male twins, not potential romantic partners
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight misdirection - his twin fascination transcends romantic purposes

jokes.text: Did someone change my name to Baskin Robbins? Because I feel like a banana split.
jokes.setup: People asking Andy to show more dance moves
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun about being split like ice cream
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad Andy pun that's painfully forced but fits his trying-too-hard personality

jokes.text: I was dancing and I did a split and I landed on my car keys in my pocket. I tore my scrotum.
jokes.setup: Andy's dancing injury explanation
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and painful injury mechanism
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with specific, cringe-worthy injury that's both absurd and believable

jokes.text: That's Michael. You're out with Michael? And Dwight. Hey-oh!
jokes.setup: Jim discovering who Pam is talking to on the phone
jokes.punchline: Realizing his groomsmen are out partying while he's handling Andy's injury
jokes.explanation: Good situational comedy showing the contrast between Jim's responsibility and others' partying

jokes.text: She'll be sitting at home saying, 'Jim, take the baby to the zoo, because I want to sit at home and eat bonbons and clip my toenails.'
jokes.setup: Michael's drunk marriage advice
jokes.punchline: His stereotypical and crude vision of married life
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael inappropriate commentary, somewhat predictable but in character

jokes.text: Now you sound like Kermit.
jokes.setup: Michael's exaggerated voice during marriage advice
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan observation about Michael sounding like Kermit the Frog
jokes.explanation: Good Jim observation with perfect economy - cuts through Michael's rambling with precise accuracy

jokes.text: Well, every little bump in the road is major pain on my scrotum.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Pam to drive more carefully
jokes.punchline: His graphic description of how driving affects his injury
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to the injury with appropriately uncomfortable detail

jokes.text: I spent the night with the bride, the night before the wedding. Oh, yeah. She stepped on my hand on her way to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Andy's suggestive claim about spending the night with Pam
jokes.punchline: The reality is completely innocent and pathetic
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke subverting expectations with Andy's pathetic reality

jokes.text: Mr. Malone, your shoes are gone. They were stolen? No, destroyed. When the bag was opened by our shoe-shine, the smell overcame him.
jokes.setup: Kevin asking about his missing shoes
jokes.punchline: Escalating revelation that they were destroyed due to smell
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with professional delivery making Kevin's foot odor a safety hazard

jokes.text: Incinerated, actually.
jokes.setup: Manager saying the shoes were destroyed
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that they required incineration
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan escalation with maximum economy - one word that makes it infinitely worse

jokes.text: Too busy knocking boots.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Dwight's messy room
jokes.punchline: Dwight's crude euphemism delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight crude boasting, functional but not particularly original

jokes.text: She made love like a tiger.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Dwight's night with Isabel
jokes.punchline: Dwight's ridiculous animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight sexual metaphor, appropriately absurd but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Look, she's a dental hygienist from Carbondale, and she makes love like one. She's a bumpkin. Pass.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Dwight should pursue Isabel
jokes.punchline: Dwight's complete reversal and snobbish dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing Dwight's pretensions and cruelty after intimacy

jokes.text: That's funny. Your wife loved this hat the last time she saw it.
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about Stanley's hat
jokes.punchline: Stanley's cutting comeback implying adultery
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stanley moment - perfectly delivered cutting comeback that's both shocking and hilarious

jokes.text: Make it softer.
jokes.setup: Angela offering Andy a pillow for his injured groin
jokes.punchline: Her awkward phrasing about making his penis area softer
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Angela's unintentionally sexual phrasing delivered innocently

jokes.text: And I have another one of them in the nude. But that one is for me.
jokes.setup: Creed describing his painted wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning his nude painting for personal use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - disturbing revelation delivered with complete casual innocence

jokes.text: Everybody see Kristy's stupid blonde extensions? It's like they were made from a plastic broom.
jokes.setup: Wedding preparation gossip
jokes.punchline: Kelly's vicious but specific insult about hair extensions
jokes.explanation: Good Kelly cattiness with a memorably specific and visual insult

jokes.text: Mom, you're totally projecting and being a drag.
jokes.setup: Pam's mom expressing wedding doubts
jokes.punchline: Pam using therapy language to shut down her mother
jokes.explanation: Good Pam moment showing growth and ability to stand up to family dynamics

jokes.text: There. Now we're even.
jokes.setup: Pam upset about her torn veil
jokes.punchline: Jim tearing his shirt/outfit to match her imperfection
jokes.explanation: Excellent romantic gesture that's both funny and touching, perfectly captures Jim's character

jokes.text: I think Pam ran away because she knew deep down she wouldn't be a good wife.
jokes.setup: Pam and Jim temporarily missing from wedding
jokes.punchline: Angela's characteristically cruel interpretation
jokes.explanation: Typical Angela meanness delivered at the worst possible moment, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Apricot. Made of real apes.
jokes.setup: Michael eating an apricot snack
jokes.punchline: His misunderstanding of the fruit's etymology
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael misunderstanding, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Those two treat the whole office like a 1970s key party.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jim and Pam's relationship
jokes.punchline: Angela's unexpected sexual reference about swinging
jokes.explanation: Surprising Angela moment with unexpectedly worldly sexual reference delivered in her judgmental style

jokes.text: Thinking about having my sperm frozen.
jokes.setup: Michael lamenting his romantic failures
jokes.punchline: His solution is to preserve his genetic material
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate overshare that's both pathetic and uncomfortable

jokes.text: I got six numbers. One more would have been a complete telephone number.
jokes.setup: Kevin bragging about his success at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Revealing he collected individual digits, not phone numbers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection joke with perfect Kevin logic - completely subverts expectations with mathematical precision

jokes.text: My Kleenex shoes were a huge conversation piece. But, man, my dogs are barking.
jokes.setup: Kevin reflecting on the wedding reception
jokes.punchline: Revealing he made shoes out of tissues and his feet hurt
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Kevin's resourceful solution to his shoe problem with predictable consequences

tmdb_overview: The Dunder Mifflin staff head to Niagara Falls for Jim and Pam's wedding, with the understanding that her pregnancy is a forbidden discussion topic, but Jim's slip of the tongue prods Michael to attempt damage control.
